title: Regime expert gates
summary: Add regime-gating features only, without adding new models or full stacking.
rationale: The current best code already benefits from controlled ensembling. This patch encodes expert-style route gates for dry, wet, testing, pit-window, late-stint, and early-race regimes as features, which may give the parent model similar regime awareness without changing architecture.
implementation_hint: Start from 001214 exactly. Add only the regime-gate feature block using the formulas below. Reuse existing or locally computed `dry_mask`, `wet_mask`, `test_mask`, `service_window_pressure`, `remaining_laps_est`, `race_progress`, `Monotone_Pit_Urgency_Proxy`, and `best_stop_now_advantage`. Define `pit_window_gate = service_window_pressure >= -0.35` (or the exact parent-equivalent threshold if already present), `late_stint_gate = remaining_laps_est <= 18 or race_progress >= 0.70`, `early_race_gate = race_progress <= 0.35`. Add `Expert_Dry_Gate_RegimeGate = dry_mask`, `Expert_WetInter_Gate_RegimeGate = wet_mask`, `Expert_Testing_Gate_RegimeGate = test_mask`, `Expert_PitWindow_Gate_RegimeGate = pit_window_gate`, `Expert_LateStint_Gate_RegimeGate = late_stint_gate`, `Expert_EarlyRace_Gate_RegimeGate = early_race_gate`, and categorical `Expert_Route_RegimeGate = select(testing, wet_late_stint, wet_general, dry_late_stint, dry_pit_window, dry_early, dry_general, other)`. Add interactions `Dry_Expert_Urgency_RegimeGate = Monotone_Pit_Urgency_Proxy*dry_gate`, `WetInter_Expert_Urgency_RegimeGate = Monotone_Pit_Urgency_Proxy*wet_gate`, `LateStint_Expert_Urgency_RegimeGate = Monotone_Pit_Urgency_Proxy*late_stint_gate`, `PitWindow_Expert_StopAdv_RegimeGate = best_stop_now_advantage*pit_window_gate`, `DryPitWindow_Expert_Interaction_RegimeGate = dry_gate*pit_window_gate`, `WetLateStint_Expert_Interaction_RegimeGate = wet_gate*late_stint_gate`. Do not add CatBoost/XGBoost/stacker changes.
expected_effect: Let the existing models specialize implicitly by race regime while keeping a single code-local feature patch.
risk: If the required urgency helpers are absent, adding them can accidentally pull in too much of the service-window block; keep helper definitions minimal.
